goal: Build an OpenPLC-in-the-loop conveyor station: openplc_runtime_attach wires an OpenPLC Runtime process to Isaac Sim via the Modbus convenience wrapper; ladder-logic coil state drives conveyor start/stop. Isaac Sim reads the run-coil register every 100 ms; belt pauses within 250 ms of the coil going LOW. Exercises openplc_runtime_attach (Phase 10 M5 P3) and diagnose_modbus_bridge. Plumbing-only canonical (no cube delivery, no robot arm).
thoughts: openplc_runtime_attach is the Phase-10 M5 P3 convenience wrapper over modbus_tcp_bridge_attach that auto-maps OpenPLC I/O coils. It uses the same subprocess pattern but separates input_map (Isaac reads from PLC outputs) from output_map (Isaac writes to PLC inputs). Register address convention: output coils at +0, input registers at +1000.

Scene: single conveyor belt on the floor, no robot arm. The belt's run_command USD attribute is mapped to OpenPLC output coil 0 (Q0.0 in IEC 61131-3 notation, Modbus holding register 0 on the default port 502).

Test workflow: (1) openplc_runtime_attach with input_map={belt_attr: 0} at rate_hz=10; (2) list_bridges confirms bridge alive; (3) diagnose_modbus_bridge checks n_register_updates>0; (4) modbus_tcp_bridge_detach cleans up. PLC process must be running externally (OpenPLC Runtime on port 502) or pymodbus mock server.

Distinct from CP-NEW-plc-conveyor (which uses raw modbus_tcp_bridge_attach). This canonical uses the openplc_runtime_attach wrapper that also configures the register address offset convention and provides a cleaner API for PLC-authoring workflows.
No simulate_args/verify_args: plumbing-only, same class as CP-NEW-plc-conveyor.
code:
# -- Lighting + ground -------------------------------------------------------
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# -- Conveyor belt: 2 m long, top surface z=0.805, belt runs in +x direction --
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/PLCConveyor",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.78],
    size=[2.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.3, 0, 0],
)

# -- OpenPLC bridge: output_map reads run-coil from PLC (register 0) ----------
# input_map drives a sensor-reset coil that the PLC reads back at register 1000.
# rate_hz=10 -> 100 ms poll, well under 250 ms reaction-time spec.
openplc_runtime_attach(
    host="127.0.0.1",
    port=502,
    output_map={"/World/PLCConveyor/run_command": 0},
    input_map={"/World/PLCConveyor/sensor_reset": 1000},
    rate_hz=10.0,
)

# -- Verify bridge is alive ---------------------------------------------------
list_bridges()
diagnose_modbus_bridge(bridge_id="openplc_127.0.0.1_502")

# -- Cleanup (call after validation) -----------------------------------------
modbus_tcp_bridge_detach(bridge_id="openplc_127.0.0.1_502")
code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_conveyor(
    prim_path={{plc_conveyor.path}},
    position={{plc_conveyor.position}},
    size={{plc_conveyor.size}},
    surface_velocity={{plc_conveyor.surface_velocity}},
)

openplc_runtime_attach(
    host={{openplc_bridge.host}},
    port={{openplc_bridge.port}},
    output_map={{openplc_bridge.output_map}},
    input_map={{openplc_bridge.input_map}},
    rate_hz={{openplc_bridge.rate_hz}},
)

list_bridges()
diagnose_modbus_bridge(bridge_id={{openplc_bridge.bridge_id}})

modbus_tcp_bridge_detach(bridge_id={{openplc_bridge.bridge_id}})
failure_modes: OpenPLC Runtime not running on port 502 — bridge attach succeeds but diagnose shows alive=false + connection_refused in log_tail. Use pymodbus mock server for offline testing.
bridge_id mismatch — list_bridges returns the actual bridge_id assigned at attach time; use that id for diagnose and detach, not a guessed value.

output_map register 0 vs 1 — OpenPLC coil Q0.0 maps to Modbus coil 0 (address 0x0001 in Modbus PDU). The openplc_runtime_attach wrapper uses holding-register semantics; confirm against PLC project's Modbus address table.
input_map offset +1000 — OpenPLC inputs (I0.x) are mapped at holding registers 1000+. Mismatched offset causes sensor_reset feedback to never reach the PLC.

Conflicting modbus_tcp_bridge_attach — if a raw modbus_tcp_bridge_attach to the same host:port is already running, openplc_runtime_attach will fail with port conflict. Call list_bridges first and detach stale bridges.
extension_notes: Upgrades CP-NEW-plc-conveyor (raw modbus_tcp_bridge_attach) to the openplc_runtime_attach convenience wrapper. Adds bidirectional I/O map (output_map + input_map) and confirms the Phase-10 M5 P3 register-offset convention (+1000 for inputs). Plumbing-only — no robot arm, no cube delivery.
